name: Python
scope: An individual at the ADVANCED proficiency level in Python can design and develop complex, scalable applications, optimizing code for performance and efficiency. They master advanced concepts like metaprogramming, decorators, context managers, concurrency (multi-threading, multi-processing, asynchronous programming), and advanced OOP principles (inheritance, polymorphism, metaclasses). Proficient with tools such as profiling and debugging utilities (cProfile, pdb), testing frameworks (unittest, pytest), CI/CD platforms (Jenkins, GitLab CI/CD), and containerization tools (Docker, Kubernetes), cloud providers like AWS and Azure, they integrate Python applications with databases, RESTful APIs, message queues, and external systems. They have used and mastered libraries like NumPy, Pandas, PyMongo, OpenCV, Scikit Learn and frameworks like Flask, Django etc. They optimize database interactions using ORMs like SQLAlchemy, have understanding of SQL and NoSQL datbases, implement best practices and design patterns, and ensure secure coding practices to mitigate vulnerabilities. Capable of refactoring legacy code for better performance and maintainability, they design scalable architectures including microservices and distributed systems, and set up automated workflows for building, testing, and deploying applications. Additionally, they lead and mentor junior developers, conduct thorough code reviews, contribute to technical documentation, and proactively improve existing systems and processes.

name: Redis
scope: A person with ADVANCED proficiency level in Redis as a competency is capable of explaining and tuning the single-threaded event-loop, multi-threaded I/O (6.x+), PSYNC2 replication backlog, diskless sync, and every persistence mode (RDB, AOF, mixed AOF-rewrite) while mapping all choices to CAP-theory trade-offs. They design and operate Redis Cluster internals—hash-slot distribution, cluster-bus gossip, failover elections—and execute zero-downtime slot rebalancing or resharding. They model data with every core and advanced structure (STRING, HASH, LIST, SET, ZSET, BITFIELD/bitmap, GEO, HLL, BLOOM/CUCKOO/TOPK, STREAM) using rigorous key naming, TTL, namespace and versioning schemes. They build high-throughput queueing on STREAMS & consumer groups, satisfy GDPR “forget”, ensure idempotency and ordering, and squeeze memory via listpack, quicklist and compression on ZSET/GEO/probabilistic sets. They run redis-benchmark, redis-cli latency, MONITOR, SLOWLOG, LATENCY DOCTOR, INFO MEMORY, k6/Locust loads, tune jemalloc, active-defrag, I/O threads, net sysctls, hugepages, hot-key tracking, LFU/LRU eviction and client-buffer limits. They deliver HA with Sentinel, Cluster, AWS ElastiCache or GCP Memorystore, orchestrate cross-AZ/region replicas, RDB/AOF snapshot pipelines, and chaos-test failure paths via Toxiproxy or Pumba to meet strict RPO/RTO. They harden security with ACLs, key-prefix patterns, protected-mode, command renames, TLS/mTLS or service-mesh sidecars, secret rotation, audit logging and SOC2/PCI/HIPAA compliance artefacts. They automate with Docker, Helm, Redis Operator on Kubernetes, Terraform/Pulumi, blue-green or rolling upgrades, Prometheus redis-exporter, Grafana/DataDog/CloudWatch dashboards, SLO alerts and multi-cloud backup/restore to S3, GCS or Azure Blob. They craft non-trivial Lua scripts (KEYS/ARGV safety) or Redis Functions 7.x, combine with MULTI/EXEC, WATCH, pipelining, and extend client libraries like Jedis, Lettuce, Redisson, Spring-Data, StackExchange.Redis or aioredis with metrics and circuit breakers. They deploy and tune modules—RedisSearch (incl. vector search), RedisJSON, RedisTimeSeries, RedisBloom—and vet third-party modules for ABI, memory and licensing concerns. They architect cache-aside, read-through, write-behind, hybrid patterns, implement SET NX EX or Redlock distributed locks, design Kafka→Redis Streams→worker CDC pipelines, embed Redis into microservice, serverless or edge tiers, and weigh cost/performance across OSS, Enterprise and cloud RAM+Flash tiers. Finally, they troubleshoot via strace, perf, eBPF, flamegraphs, repair AOF/RDB corruption, write post-mortems, mentor teams and set Redis usage standards that continuously shrink MTTR and latency budgets.
long_scope:
ADVANCED-LEVEL REDIS COMPETENCY SCOPE  
(6-10 years of focused, hands-on experience)

1. Core Architectural & Theoretical Mastery  
• Explain in detail Redis’ event-loop architecture, single-threaded command execution, multi-threaded I/O (6.x+) and how that influences latency, throughput, and CPU usage.  
• Articulate the full replication pipeline (PSYNC2, diskless sync, backlog, partial resync) and the persistence pipeline (RDB, AOF, AOF-rewrite, mixed-AOF) together with fsync trade-offs.  
• Apply CAP-theory implications for Redis (eventual consistency under async replication, WAIT for synchronous durability, strong vs. causal ordering).  
• Calculate hash-slot distribution, cluster-bus gossip, and how cluster state is maintained, including failover elections and slot-to-node mapping.

2. Data Modeling & Advanced Data Structures  
• Select and combine core structures (STRING, HASH, LIST, SET, ZSET, BITFIELD/bitmap, GEO, HLL, BLOOM, STREAM) according to access-pattern, memory profile, and mutation rate.  
• Design schema-less, namespaced key spaces with rigorous naming conventions, versioning, and TTL strategies that fit multi-tenant, multiregion, or GDPR “right to forget” requirements.  
• Engineer high-throughput job/queueing systems using STREAMS & consumer groups, handling idempotency, reprocessing, and message ordering nuances.  
• Optimize ZSET & GEO queries with lexicographic ranges, score bucketing, and memory compaction (listpack / quicklist).  
• Plan and implement probabilistic datasets (HLL, Bloom, Cuckoo) to meet accuracy & memory budgets.

3. Performance Engineering  
• Run and interpret redis-benchmark, redis-cli --latency, MONITOR, SLOWLOG, LATENCY DOCTOR, INFO LATENCY & MEMORY to diagnose spikes, drift, or fragmentation.  
• Develop repeatable load-test suites (k6, JMeter, Locust) that mimic production pipelining, batching, and TLS cost.  
• Tune allocator (jemalloc, glibc, tcmalloc) parameters, act on fragmentation ratios, and use active defragmentation.  
• Configure I/O threads, net.core sysctls, NIC interrupt coalescing, and hugepages to reach sub-millisecond P99.  
• Implement hot-key detection & mitigation (keyspace notifications, tracking, client-side sharding or partitioning).  
• Choose and justify eviction policies (LRU, LFU, volatile-*, TTL-based) for bounded-memory deployments.

4. Reliability, High Availability & Disaster Recovery  
• Deploy, operate, and troubleshoot Redis Sentinel, Redis Cluster, and vendor/cloud equivalents (AWS ElastiCache cluster mode, Azure Cache Enterprise, GCP Memorystore Cluster).  
• Orchestrate resharding and live cluster slot re-balancing with redis-cli --cluster or redis-trib without customer-visible downtime.  
• Design cross-AZ / cross-region replication topologies, evaluate network RTT impacts, and create RPO/RTO-driven backup strategies (RDB shipping, continuous AOF, cloud snapshot APIs).  
• Simulate, measure, and document failover cascade (data divergence, split-brain, fencing) in staging using Chaos-Engineering approaches (e.g., Toxiproxy, Pumba).  
• Implement connection-handling safeguards (client-output-buffer-limit, TCP backlog, busy-replica-kill) to prevent cascading failure.

5. Security, Governance & Compliance  
• Configure and audit ACLs (users, categories, command renames, key-prefix patterns).  
• Implement TLS (stunnel / built-in TLS) with cipher-suite hardening, mutual auth, and rotation.  
• Enforce protected-mode, timeout limits, renaming or disabling dangerous commands (EVAL, KEYS, CONFIG).  
• Embed Redis into zero-trust or service-mesh (mTLS sidecars) environments; manage firewall rules, network policies, and private-link endpoints.  
• Produce compliance artefacts (backup encryption, audit logging, secret management) for SOC2 / PCI / HIPAA scenarios.

6. Operational Excellence & DevOps Automation  
• Containerize Redis with fine-tuned Dockerfiles, build Helm charts or use Redis Operator for Kubernetes with persistent volumes, anti-affinity rules, PodDisruptionBudgets, and readiness probes.  
• Automate provisioning in Terraform / Pulumi (parameter groups, subnet groups, ACLs, global datastores).  
• Implement blue/green or rolling upgrades across minor/patch versions with replica promotion & deterministic seed lists.  
• Integrate Prometheus Redis-Exporter, Grafana dashboards, DataDog integrations, CloudWatch metrics; craft actionable SLOs / alerts (latency, hit ratio, replication delay, mem frag).  
• Operate backup pipelines (S3, GCS, Azure Blob), use redis-check-rdb/aof integrity tools, and periodically execute point-in-time restore drills.

7. Scripting & Programmability  
• Write non-trivial Lua scripts: safe key access with KEYS & ARGV, sha caching, time complexity analysis; detect and avoid script replication pitfalls & long-running script watchdogs.  
• Migrate to Redis Functions (7.x) for better sandboxing and failover semantics.  
• Combine scripts with pipelining / transactions (MULTI/EXEC) for atomic read-modify-write patterns, handling WATCH / CAS contention.  
• Build client libraries or extend existing ones (Jedis, Lettuce, Redisson, Spring-Data, StackExchange.Redis, aioredis) with circuit-breakers, automatic reconnect, and metrics hooks.

8. Modules & Ecosystem Proficiency  
• Deploy and operate official modules:  
  – RedisSearch / RediSearch: create/alter indexes, numeric/filter clauses, vector similarity search, and aggregate pipelines.  
  – RedisJSON: hierarchical JSON manipulation, path queries, combined Search+JSON indexing.  
  – RedisTimeSeries: down-sampling, retention, compaction rules, multi-series range queries.  
  – RedisBloom (Bloom, Cuckoo, TopK, CountMin) – cardinality/approximate counting.  
• Evaluate third-party modules’ memory footprint, licensing, and ABI compatibility.  
• Instrument module metrics, plan resource allocation (threads per shard, fork model), and coordinate module upgrades with core engine versions.

9. Integration & Solution Architecture  
• Architect Cache-Aside, Read-Through, Write-Behind, and Hybrid subsystems; compare with CDN, Memcached, or local caches for full stack strategy.  
• Implement distributed locking (SET NX EX / Redlock) with clear documentation of edge cases, clock drift assumptions, and failure semantics.  
• Design data-ingestion or change-data-capture pipelines (Kafka►Redis Streams►Workers►DB), ensuring at-least-once or exactly-once semantics where feasible.  
• Embed Redis in microservice, serverless, or event-driven architectures, advising on connection pooling, stateless services, and autoscaling behaviour.  
• Provide cost/performance trade-off analysis for OSS vs. Enterprise vs. Cloud-managed licensing, memory tiering (RAM+Flash), or multi-tenant clusters.

10. Troubleshooting, Root-Cause Analysis & Communication  
• Trace and resolve issues spanning client libraries, networking (Nagle, TCP keepalive), OS limits, and disk/FS throughput (IOPS vs bandwidth) using tools like strace, perf, eBPF, flamegraphs.  
• Perform forensic analysis of AOF/RDB corruption, replication loops, or data drift; create incident timelines, diffs, and remediation plans.  
• Communicate findings to stakeholders, write post-mortems, propose systemic fixes (back-pressure, retries, config guards).  
• Mentor teams, establish coding standards for Redis usage, and run knowledge-sharing sessions or internal workshops.

Outcome Expectations for an ADVANCED Practitioner  
• Runs production Redis installations exceeding 100K ops/sec with <1 ms P99 latency, 99.99 % availability, and audited security posture.  
• Delivers authoritative guidance on cluster design, memory sizing, persistence strategy, and cost optimisation that survives architectural reviews.  
• Automates end-to-end CI/CD pipelines for Redis upgrades, backups, and failover drills, reducing MTTR for cache-layer incidents by ≥50 %.  
• Coaches engineers on effective Redis usage, pre-empts anti-patterns, and liaises with platform teams to standardise observability, security and scaling procedures.
